ai_summary: Elon Musk announces that a Falcon 9 booster has completed its 34th flight, demonstrating SpaceX's progress toward rocket reusability and cost reduction through repeated launches of the same hardware. This milestone illustrates the engineering and operational achievement of designing rockets for multiple flight cycles, a core business strategy for reducing launch costs and improving space transportation economics.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) March 31, 2026

summary: In 4 or 5 years, there will be a launch every hour

This reflects SpaceX's manufacturing and reusability strategy to dramatically increase launch frequency, demonstrating how operational scaling and engineering optimization can transform industry economics—a key lesson for operators building capital-intensive, high-throughput businesses.

text:
Custom orders of the Tesla Model S & X have come to an end. All that’s left are some in inventory.

We will have an official ceremony to mark the ending of an era. I love those cars. 

This was me at production launch 14 years ago: https://t.co/6kvCf9HTHc
summary: Custom orders of the Tesla Model S & X have come to an end. All that’s left are some in inventory. We will have an official ceremony to mark the ending of an era. I love those cars. This was me at production launch 14 years ago:
ai_summary: Tesla is discontinuing custom orders for Model S and X, transitioning to inventory-only sales while marking the end of an era with a ceremony. This signals a significant shift in Tesla's production strategy and product lifecycle management, reflecting decisions about resource allocation, manufacturing focus, and how established product lines are managed as the company evolves its portfolio.

text:
Indeed, it was *because* I was not from the aerospace industry that SpaceX made such radical breakthroughs. Same for Tesla. 

Those in the industry would have if they could have. https://t.co/MYzMLrpOhh
summary: Indeed, it was *because* I was not from the aerospace industry that SpaceX made such radical breakthroughs. Same for Tesla. Those in the industry would have if they could have.
ai_summary: Elon Musk argues that his outsider status to aerospace and automotive industries enabled SpaceX and Tesla to achieve radical breakthroughs that established industry players could not or would not attempt. The core business insight is that industry incumbents are often constrained by existing paradigms, capital structures, and risk-aversion, while entrepreneurs without industry baggage can pursue unconventional approaches to solve fundamental problems—a lesson applicable to how outsider perspectives can drive disruptive innovation in any mature industry.

text: Neuralink enables people with ALS to speak again https://t.co/jGLiUjRT1R
summary: Neuralink enables people with ALS to speak again
ai_summary: Elon Musk announced a Neuralink milestone: their brain-computer interface successfully enabled a person with ALS (amyotrophic lateral sclerosis) to speak again, demonstrating functional progress in the company's neural implant technology. This represents a significant product validation moment for Neuralink, showing real-world medical application of their core technology and advancement toward their stated mission of restoring communication and mobility to paralyzed patients.
